title: Benalmadena Catamaran Charter: Costs, Seasons & Best Boats for 2026
html:
Benalmadena, on Spain's Costa del Sol, offers superb catamaran charter options departing from Puerto Marina. Prices for a full-day crewed catamaran typically range from 1,200 to 2,500 EUR. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators for a hassle-free experience.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Benalmadena?

A full-day crewed catamaran charter in Benalmadena costs between 1,200 and 2,500 EUR. Prices vary by boat size, season, and included extras like food and drink. Half-day trips start at around 700 EUR for smaller catamarans. For the best value, you can rent a boat directly through BoatHire24 and compare verified listings.

  • Shared catamaran tour per person: 50-80 EUR
  • Small motorboat half-day (e.g. Axopar): 250-400 EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (e.g. Lagoon 42): 1,200-1,800 EUR
  • Crewed luxury yacht (e.g. Azimut 60): 2,000-4,000 EUR

Where to set off in Benalmadena

Most charters depart from Puerto Marina Benalmadena, a modern marina with full facilities. Other nearby departure points include Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, 8 nautical miles south, and the larger Puerto José Banús in Marbella, 20 nautical miles down the coast. Popular anchorages include Calahonda Bay and the sheltered waters off Torremolinos. For day trips, many boats head to the island of Isla de las Palomas or the picturesque town of Mijas Costa.

Which boat to rent

  1. Catamaran, ideal for spacious, stable family outings; up to 12 guests; from 700 EUR half-day
  2. Motor Yacht, perfect for speed and luxury; 8-10 guests; from 1,500 EUR full-day
  3. Sailing Monohull, for those who love traditional sailing; 6-8 guests; from 400 EUR half-day
  4. RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat), best for water sports and small groups; up to 6 guests; from 200 EUR half-day
  5. Luxury Gulet, a classic Turkish design now available; 10-12 guests; from 2,000 EUR full-day

When choosing, consider the Zambezia Leopard 53ft, a popular crewed catamaran. For full details, review this catamaran listing on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to charter a boat in Benalmadena?

In Spain, you may need a licence for bareboat charter, but crewed charters require none. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. For 2026, regulations remain based on the Spanish Recreational Navigation Law; a day skipper licence (PER) is often needed for boats over 15 metres, but most Benalmadena catamaran charters include a captain.

Local tip: Book a late-morning departure to enjoy calm seas and gentle winds. The afternoon Levante breeze picks up around 1400, adding a refreshing swish to your return sail.

Seasons for catamaran charter in Benalmadena

High season runs June to September, with July and August being the busiest. The prevailing wind is the Levante, from the east, bringing dry heat. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and fewer crowds, with excellent visibility. Winter charters are possible but cooler; many smaller operators close from November to February.

At a glance

  • Price band: 50 EUR shared to 4,000 EUR luxury charter
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to full-day
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Benalmadena

Can I rent a catamaran without a licence in Benalmadena?

Yes, you can rent a crewed catamaran without any licence, as the skipper holds the required qualifications.

What is the best time of day for a catamaran trip?

Morning departures around 1000 are ideal, offering calm seas and a relaxed pace before the afternoon breeze picks up.

Are children allowed on catamaran charters?

Most catamarans welcome children, with onboard safety equipment and ample space, but confirm with the operator when booking.

How far can you sail from Benalmadena in a day?

A full-day catamaran can reach Marbella (20 nautical miles) or even Gibraltar (40 nautical miles) and back, weather permitting.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Benalmadena and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
